label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3

extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam won his second consecutive sprint via perfect late-race positioning from van Poppel, beating Pedersen and McLay after Ackermann faded from an early jump.
tactical_patterns: Van Poppel's late release at 200m proved superior to Trek's extended color-order leadout, Bennett emerging from apparent invisibility.
Ackermann's premature acceleration allowed Bennett to exploit slipstream differential before launching, causing Pascal to fade before line.
Final corner at 750m unwound late enough that sustained leadout trains neutralized—late positioning trump organized trains in compressed finales.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: Two consecutive wins suggests tactical maturity in positioning behind van Poppel's perfectly-timed late releases rather than leadout dependency.
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: His dual role delivering Bennett to victories while finishing tenth reveals exceptional leadout judgment on timing and placement.
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Initiated sprint too early despite looking lively, fading before line suggests poor tactical calibration on 750m finishing straight.
rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: negative: Out of position on pancake-flat sprint finish indicates positioning deficiency or team support weakness, concerning for future flat sprints.
rider_signals.COQUARD Bryan: neutral: Diamond formation trap suggests strong legs but poor positioning awareness in compressed final 200 meters.
